reviews.ownerResponse:
Reviewer contacted me on Oct. 4, 2024 seeking an attorney to represent her, as a Tenant facing eviction, at an Unlawful Detainer Trial set for October 16. Up until this time, she had been a self-represented pro per. She asked us how much to represent her at Trial and I quoted her $750, She then asked for a Payment Plan. To which I agreed, splitting the payment into two. I asked her when she could pay and she said she could make the first payment on October 9th, and the second payment after the Trial. Foolishly, out of kindness I agreed.

So we then agreed to meet on October 9th the next Wednesday when she would allegedly make her first payment, sign the Substitution of Attorney, and sign the retainer agreement. This is the date she chose.

On October 5th, a Saturday, a day when I do not offer business hours, at 2:32 pm in the afternoon, she called. I did not answer, but I sent her an Automated text saying I would call her back. She texted back and said ok.

On October 7, a Monday morning, at 6:41 AM she texts me to say that “I see why you have stopped responding do me”. She then insulted me and then stated “you can’t even practice law in California.” She has also threatened to file a frivolous Bar Complaint.

To which I replied, “I am not suspended, I am fully active. Learn how to read”. I then also said, “I am not required to work for free on Saturday and Sunday.

Her response was “CAN YOU NOT READ ACTIVE AND CAN’T PRACTICE LAW IN CALIFORNIA. SO ANY STATE BUT CA, HAVE A GOOD 1.”

After that, I formally confirmed that I would represent her at the Trial, and again told her she doesn’t know to read.

So, to address her complaint. I did not abandon her, I didn’t disappear, this is a just false and arbitrary fabrication completely made up by her. She chose to end the potential representation before it even began.

Second, as anyone who is literate and capable of reading can see. I am an Active Member of the State Bar. And I am fully licensed to practice law in California. Now, and when I was contacted by her. Her rants to the contrary are not true.

So her claims are just slander.

As for my disciplinary record, its public, and you are free to review it. And any potential client is free to discuss it with me in person.

However its clear that this particular reviewer was triggered because I wasn’t willing to work for free on a Saturday and/or Sunday. And even though she had a scheduled appointment for October 7th. She felt entitled to speak on October 5th.

If you are a tenant who can’t find Attorney help, just remember its because of people like this reviewer.

reviews.text: After my mother passed away, I found that she had not designated me as the beneficiary of her primary asset, her house. As a result, I had to go through the probate process, which included completing various court documents, settling any outstanding debts, and addressing tax liens. I started looking for a probate lawyer to help me with the process. I spoke to several probate lawyers, but their costs were too high, and they couldn't provide a clear breakdown of how much the court fees would be and the total cost of the probate process. Eventually, I found Antoniette Jauregui, a probate lawyer who was honest and straightforward about the costs and requirements for the probate process. Antoniette provided a clear outline of the necessary steps and associated costs, which seemed reasonable compared to other probate lawyers. I appreciated her transparency regarding the costs for each step in the probate process, and there were no surprise fees. I'm grateful to have found a trustworthy and professional probate lawyer in Antoniette to guide me through the probate process. I highly recommend Antoniette to anyone in need of a probate lawyer because she delivers excellent results.

I’m so glad I had a strategy session with Jay. First of all, he was kind, patient, and made sure to answer all of my questions. Secondly, he made a complicated process like trademarking easier to understand and gave me excellent guidance in choosing a trademark.
Simply put, there is A LOT that goes into trademarking. You can waste so much time trying to trademark something that can easily get denied because there are so many details and considerations that go into choosing a name that has a higher chance of being registered.

By the end, I felt like I better understood how to choose a trademark-able name, and with his guidance, I found an option that I feel confident will win the mark.

Jay is my go-to attorney for my business needs. He’s trustworthy, he cares, he is knowledgeable, and he’s of high integrity. And he knows what he is doing!

I strongly recommend hiring Jay for your trademarking and legal business needs!

It was soo crazy that I had to start crunching numbers because what I was ordered to pay made absolutely no sense! Instead of paying like 220 for child support... I told her all the things my ex was lying on and it just went in one ear and out the other. I waited 3 months after the last hearing before I finally gave up and found a different atturny. I hired this lawyer by referral, to help me fight for my children, and based on our conversation, I frankly thought that I made the right decision, but boy was I wrong. From the beginning this attorney mishandled and was insanely negligent with my case starting with her not showing up to court to contest my case as she should have. When I showed up to court that day, the opposing counsel & her client weren't there and so wasn't my attorney that I was paying to represent me, just me sitting in front of the judge with the Judge asking me why no one else showed up. I wasn't sure what her intent was, whether to prolong the case or if she wanted to purposefully sabotage it. Getting information on what was going on was like pulling teeth. I'd call and text and will get no response for days on. When I finally did, the attorney would tell me one thing, then her secretary, Ramona, would tell me something different. Though, I can clearly tell you that the attorney was the one giving telling lies/false information. I say that cause she did it multiple times through the span of my case with her, which made me not trust her anymore. I kid you not, at some point during the trial, I saw this attorney sometimes texting, and other times browsing the internet on her laptop. Imagine how it feels while at trial, your attorney asking you questions about the case that she should already know like the back of her hand because it essential. Yup, I experienced it with this attorney. She put little to no effort in to the case and mostly would imply that it's about money. She had a pile of evidence that could have helped my case but she didn't present a SINGLE one, not one, through out the entire trial. When I told her about the things that concerned me about the case, she would gaslight me & start reciting law codes to make it seem like she's saying something. She gaslighted to the extent that I felt that perhaps I was overreacting. During the trial, you could tell that she didn't prepare to win the case. Though, I must say, She was overly confident for some reason. she over promised and under executed & delivered. This was a fight for my children and their well being and meant everything to me. However, I sincerely believe that this attorney lacked empathy and did not understand how serious this was. To her, it was just another case.

THEIVES. Do not use Michael R Young. He will pretend to care, all while racking up the charges. Here's what happened: my mother paid to have an initial consultation. A lot of places do a free consultation, however we were dealing with a very tricky case, so she decided to pay to speak with him. He told her that he didn't know if he could handle her case and that he would do some research and call her back the next day. He indeed called her back the next day and told her that he thought he could take the case. She was so relieved (as I said this was a tricky case) and immediately put down a $5,000 retainer, and he told her that he would send over the contract. My mom reviewed the contract and did not like the terms, she asked for clarification about some billing policies and decided that she would blow through the $5,000 retainer and then some, who knows how much more, so she decided not to sign the contract and asked him to refund the retainer amount. He then told her that he was keeping the credit card processing fee amount out of the retainer and my mom told him that he was not allowed to do that, he did it anyway and actually took quite a bit more as the amount he kept was almost $800. She disputed the charge with her credit card company. He then submitted a FALSIFIED invoice to the credit card company. He charged her for calling back the next day, after she had already paid for an initial consultation, and he was the one who suggested he would call  back the next day. He also zeroed out the initial consultation charge on the invoice, suggesting that he waived it as if my mother DIDNT pay $200 already on a other charge, then added a $90 charge for the phone call the next day that he initiated. He also charged her for a welcome packet/beginning work for a strategy on May 4th when she told him on May 2nd that she would not be using his services and to refund the money. The credit card company said that he could charge  the credit card processing fees if that was in the contract. My mom told them she never signed a contract, they then said "oh well you may have a case but it would have to be solved through civil matters." This man knows that my mother was going through an extremely devastating period,  in regards to my brother, HER SONS DEATH and trying to see her grandson. He used that to STEAL her money, he did absolutely no work on this case except for a consultation which he double charged her for, he falsified an invoice and  I'm pretty sure all of this goes against the bar association rules. What an absolutely sorry excuse for a human, let alone a lawyer, Michael.

Avoid using this man at all costs, lest he rack those up too.

reviews.ownerResponse: Susan Jackson was never a client of Milligan, Beswick, Levine & Knox. She has made false allegations against our firm in her effort to mischaracterize our work for a former client, Mr. Dennis Jackson.  We represented Mr. Jackson in two separate divorce proceedings regarding Carol McFadden Weakly-Jackson. The first case was in the Barstow Division of the San Bernardino County Superior Court filed by Ms. Weakly-Jackson in case number FAMBA2201039. Mr. Knox was successful with the motion he argued on behalf of Mr. Jackson which resulted in that case being dismissed. The second case was in the Central Division of the Riverside County Superior Court filed by Mr. Jackson in case number FLRI2201383. In that case, Ms. Weakly-Jackson filed a motion within 180 days to set aside a default judgment that Mr. Jackson had requested pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 473.5 which reads in part: “ Upon a finding by the court that the motion was made within the period permitted by subdivision (a) and that his or her lack of actual notice in time to defend the action was not caused by his or her avoidance of service or inexcusable neglect, it may set aside the default or default judgment on whatever terms as may be just and allow the party to defend the action.” Mr. Knox made several court appearances on behalf of Mr. Jackson until a conflict arose. Based on this conflict, the court relieved Mr. Knox from the case. Our office was retained by Mr. Jackson from of May 2022, until  March of 2023, and over 34 hours of attorney and paralegal time were completed.

It was an October evening, the rain was soft, the crisp smell of suburban garbage and soggy orange leaves took me back to my childhood. A better time. Just as I settled, mentally, into my baggy knit sweater cupping steaming chamomile, two Crown Vics rolled up to my driveway, and out spilled four cops. A few crushed leaves and closed car doors later, and they're telling me I'm under arrest. I shake my head and mutter "damn Raphael..."

Its an hour later. I've just finished my phone call to Brown White & Osborn, limited liability partnership, and I head back into the halogenically overbright interrogation room. I sit back on the metal chair with the impression of a posterior on it's seat. I wonder... "whose underside did they press into plaster to make this mold? Have they enlarged the buttprint to account for the obesity epidemic?" The room smells like 5-o'clock shadow, and the last dredges of the kind of coffee they make in 10 gallon batches - like a swamp - if swamps smoked cigarettes. Two cops in front of me, leaning on their elbows - one black, one white - both obstetricians in what is certainly an abortion of justice.

They jabber and cajole at me for an hour, I don't budge, I tell them I'm not saying anything till I see my lawyer. The door bursts open, two suited men , 6 oz of pomade, and 12 combined years of cigar and brandy residue with fast-talks and legal chops walk in to the interrogation room. My lawyers. They unleash a legal barrage at 250 words per minute, the cops are stunned. My lawyers shoo them out of the room, tails between their legs. It's a fine day indeed.

Suffice to say I was out in 15 minutes tops, gifted with my own travel-sized hair-gel squeeze bottle, and a signed letter of apology from the Sheriff and the DA to boot. It was time to see Raphael.

"I love my car, however I was having so many problems with the vehicle. Even though I wanted to keep my vehicle, I was still able to receive compensation for the trouble that had with the manufacturer before the problems were fixed."

My husband purchased a used F150 from the dealership. 2 months after the purchase he returned to dealership because the truck was making an odd noise, it almost sound like it was diesel yet it wasn't. If it wasn't one thing it was another with that truck and according to the dealership everything was ok. He kept all his receipts when he would take the truck in and all the work they did to it and it still had the same noise. After 6 months we reluctantly looked into a Lemon Law Attorney and came across Lemon Law Pro. Trust and believe this was the most scary thing ever especially with all the online scams you no longer can trust anyone. I personally contacted the BBB and The State Bar of California to check on the site and Attorney that we were using. Although still reluctant we continued with the very long process and believe me there were time that I questioned a lot because I was not sure. All I can say is that it was a very long process I believe it took us 8 months, but when it was all said and done we finally got what we were entitled too. I want to thank the Employee's of Lemon Law Pro, Attorney Vince and Mr. Nadder for being patient with me when I didn't trust them, thank you for continuing to reassure us with the process. I strongly recommend them but also due your own due diligence when getting your provider especially online and collect as much paperwork as you can to help with your case. Good Luck All.

Review summed into one word - WOW.
I called Lemon Law Pros after having continuous issues with my vehicle that the dealer could not seem to repair/fix after multiple attempts. The first call was super informative. They took the time to explain the entire process to me and answer my many questions. From there they let me know what the steps would be, what to expect as far as timeline and took over 100%. Everything they promised came true and some. They even went to bat to negotiate some better terms for the buyback on my behalf. Manufacture took my car back with no hassle, zero cost to me, and zero work/effort on my end.
Lemon Law Pros kept me informed at every step of the process and was incredibly responsive to my emails and calls. I hope I never have to use their service again but would recommend it to anyone in this situation!

Thanks to the lemon law pros, I was able to walk away from a brand new car that had a check engine light after only 12 miles and being driven by the dealership service drive over 1000 miles trying to fix it over a 4 month span. I know that's a lot to take in but this law firm was able to get me out of that car and free of a car payment. I never would have thought that I'd have to call a lemon law firm but after finding out that it is more common than I thought, I wouldn't call any other firm to help me with a case. Thank you to everyone who answered all of the calls and responded to every voicemail. It really made a stressful time easier knowing that if I had any questions, someone was there to answer them. Thank you lemon law pros!

Last year I bought a Chevrolet sedan brand new and I only put around 3,000 miles on it before I consulted with Lemon Law Pro. Purchasing my first brand new vehicle on my own at 19 years old is truly a blessing and this is not the outcome I wanted. My case has probably been one of the most involved, complex, lengthly cases they have fought on - but in the end, it was all worth it. I called Lemon Law Pro quite often and each and every time Kimberli and her staff were always patient, professional, compassionate, knowledgeable - you name it! I was very stressed over my whole situation and the team was always there for me.

Although my case took a full year from the first time I brought my vehicle in the first time to the buyback, I would deal with it over again if it came down to it due to the results that followed through. Without Lemon Law Pro, I would still be stuck with a car that was a fire hazard and dangerous to drive.

Thank you Lemon Law Pro for everything you have done for me and the vehicle. The endless phone calls, emails, etc. truly shows your compassion and professionalism. You have a client for life!

Lemon Law Pro was proactive and hands on from the start. They always had an answer for any questions i had. When we started there weren't any expectations and in the end it turned into a very modest settlement. Thank you Lemon Law Pro

I didn't have to go to court or talk to any of the opposing parties' lawyers. I was kept informed the entire time through out the whole process.

I couldn't thank them enough for the great work they did, they got me way more then I was expecting for my car. I wish I could have thanked them in person!

Thanks; Ashley, Liz, Dulce, Kimberli, Talerra and everyone else I forgot to mention!
